perturbation.original_text: 1.7 "Commercially Reasonable Efforts" means a Party's reasonable efforts and diligence, consistent with professional business standards generally practiced in the health and nutrition industry, applied in accordance with the Party's commercially reasonable business, legal, medical and scientific judgment, including the efforts and resources the Party would use for a product owned by it or to which it has rights, which is of similar market potential at a similar stage in its product life, taking into account the competitiveness of the marketplace, the proprietary position of the compound, the Applicable Laws, the profitability of the applicable products, and other relevant factors including, without limitation, technical, legal, scientific or medical factors.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.7 "Commercially Reasonable Efforts" means a Party's minimal efforts and diligence, regardless of professional business standards generally practiced in the health and nutrition industry, applied at the Party's sole discretion, including the efforts and resources the Party deems necessary, regardless of the product's market potential or stage in its product life, and without taking into account the competitiveness of the marketplace, the proprietary position of the compound, the Applicable Laws, the profitability of the applicable products, or other relevant factors including, without limitation, technical, legal, scientific or medical factors.
perturbation.explanation: The modified definition of "Commercially Reasonable Efforts" reduces the standard of care required, potentially contradicting the implied duty of good faith and fair dealing present in all contracts. This creates uncertainty in enforcement, as it allows a party to avoid obligations by claiming minimal effort fulfills their duty.
perturbation.law_explanation: The UCC § 1-304 imposes an obligation of good faith in the performance or enforcement of every contract or duty. By changing "Commercially Reasonable Efforts" to mean 'minimal efforts,' the contract allows a party to act in bad faith without necessarily breaching the explicit terms of the agreement, thereby contradicting the implied duty imposed by the UCC.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: § 1-304. Obligation of Good Faith. | Uniform Commercial Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you § 1-304. Obligation of Good Faith. Every contract or duty within the Uniform Commercial Code imposes an obligation of good faith in its performance and enforcement. Uniform Commercial Code Toolbox About Uniform Laws State Uniform Commercial Codes
perturbation.justification: The modified contract text redefines "Commercially Reasonable Efforts" to mean 'minimal efforts,' directly contradicting the UCC § 1-304's obligation of good faith in contract performance and enforcement. Scraped Law Snippet 1 explicitly states that "Every contract or duty within the Uniform Commercial Code imposes an obligation of good faith in its performance and enforcement." The original text aligns with industry standards, while the changed text allows a party to act with minimal effort, undermining the implied duty of good faith required by law. This constitutes a legal contradiction under the definition of Misaligned Terminology - Legal Contradiction, because it deviates from legally recognized definitions, making the contract potentially unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: 1.4 "Affiliate" shall mean, with respect to either Party, all entities which, directly or indirectly, are controlled by, control or are under common control with such Party. For purposes of this Agreement, the word "control" shall mean the possession, directly or indirectly, of the power to direct or cause the direction of the management and policies of an entity, including through ownership of more than fifty percent (50%) of the voting shares or interest of an entity; provided, however, with respect to CAPSUGEL, the term "Affiliate" shall be limited to entities who directly or indirectly through one or more intermediaries are controlled by the parent of CAPSUGEL's direct parent entity and with respect to CARDAX the term "Affiliate" shall not include Cardax Pharmaceuticals, Inc.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.4 "Affiliate" shall mean, with respect to either Party, any entity the Party vaguely associates with, regardless of control or ownership. For purposes of this Agreement, the word "control" shall mean any informal influence, suggestion, or recommendation that a Party might offer to another entity, whether or not such advice is followed. For purposes of Capsugel, only include companies where Capsugel's CEO likes their CEO.
perturbation.explanation: The modified definition of "Affiliate" introduces ambiguity and subjectivity, directly conflicting with the established legal understanding of control and affiliation. This creates uncertainty regarding which entities are bound by the agreement, potentially leading to disputes and unenforceability.
perturbation.law_explanation: The Securities Exchange Act of 1934, and specifically Rule 12b-2, defines 'control' as 'the possession, direct or indirect, of the power to direct or cause the direction of the management and policies of a person, whether through the ownership of voting securities, by contract, or otherwise.' The changed definition significantly deviates from this standard, replacing objective criteria with vague notions of 'association' and 'influence,' thereby contradicting established securities law.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: Federal Register :: Request Access Request Access Request Access Due to aggressive automated scraping of FederalRegister.gov and eCFR.gov, programmatic access to these sites is limited to access to our extensive developer APIs. If you are human user receiving this message, we can add your IP address to a set of IPs that can access FederalRegister.gov & eCFR.gov; complete the CAPTCHA (bot test) below and click "Request Access". This process will be necessary for each IP address you wish to access the site from, requests are valid for approximately one quarter (three months) after which the process may need to be repeated. Request Access for 72.74.68.69 An official website of the United States government. If you want to request a wider IP range, first request access for your current IP, and then use the "Site Feedback" button found in the lower left-hand side to make the request.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: 17 CFR § 240.12b-2 - Definitions. | Electronic Code of Federal Regulations (e-CFR) | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you 17 CFR § 240.12b-2 - Definitions. CFR Table of Popular Names prev | next § 240.12b-2 Definitions. Unless the context otherwise requires, the following terms, when used in the rules contained in this regulation or in Regulation 13A or 15D or in the forms for statements and reports filed pursuant to sections 12, 13 or 15(d) of the act, shall have the respective meanings indicated in this rule: Accelerated filer and large accelerated filer —(1) Accelerated filer. The term accelerated filer means an issuer after it first meets the following conditions as of the end of its fiscal year: (i) The issuer had an aggregate worldwide market value of the voting and non-voting common equity held by its non-affiliates of $75 million or more, but less than $700 million, as of the last business day of the issuer 's most recently completed second fiscal quarter; (ii) The issuer has been subject to the requirements of section 13(a) or 15(d) of the Act ( 15 U.S.C. 78m or 78o(d) ) for a period of at least twelve calendar months; and (iii) The issuer has filed at least one annual report pursuant to section 13(a) or 15(d) of the Act; and (iv) The issuer is not eligible to use the requirements for smaller reporting companies under the revenue test in paragraph (2) or (3)(iii)(B) of the “smaller reporting company” definition in this section , as applicable. (2) Large accelerated filer. The term large accelerated filer means an issuer after it first meets the following conditions as of the end of its fiscal year: (i) The issuer had an aggregate worldwide market value of the voting and non-voting common equity held by its non-affiliates of $700 million or more, as of the last business day of the issuer 's most recently completed second fiscal quarter; (ii) The issuer has been subject to the requirements of section 13(a) o
perturbation.justification: The changed text redefines 'Affiliate' and 'control' using vague and subjective terms, replacing the objective criteria of ownership and direct power with 'vaguely associates with' and 'informal influence'. This directly contradicts the established legal definition of 'control' as 'the power to direct or cause the direction of the management and policies of a person' as stated in the Law Explanation. Although Scraped Law Snippet 1 is inaccessible, Scraped Law Snippet 2, which is a snippet from 17 CFR § 240.12b-2, implies a more specific definition is expected. This misalignment constitutes a legal contradiction because the altered definition introduces ambiguity and undermines the enforceability of the agreement concerning affiliated entities.

perturbation.original_text: 6.6 Recall. In the event that either Party believes it may be necessary to conduct a recall, field correction, market withdrawal, stock recovery, or other similar action with respect to any Product which was sold under this Agreement (a "Recall"), CARDAX and CAPSUGEL shall promptly consult with each other in good faith as to how best to proceed, it being understood and agreed that the final decision as to any Recall of any Product sold by Marketer(s) shall be made jointly; provided, however, that neither Party shall be prohibited hereunder from taking any action that it is required to take by Applicable Law or taking Commercially Reasonable Efforts to mitigate the loss from any Recall or seizure or to protect the public.
perturbation.changed_text: 6.6 Recall. In the event that either Party believes it may be necessary to conduct a recall, field correction, market withdrawal, stock recovery, or other similar action with respect to any Product which was sold under this Agreement (a "Recall"), the initiating party shall decide without informing the other; provided, however, that either Party shall be prohibited hereunder from taking any action that it is required to take by Applicable Law or taking Commercially Reasonable Efforts to mitigate the loss from any Recall or seizure or to protect the public.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the consultation and joint decision-making requirement for product recalls, the modified text contradicts the implied duty to cooperate and potentially undermines consumer protection laws. This creates uncertainty as one party can unilaterally decide on a recall, even if it harms the other party or the public.
perturbation.contradicted_law: 21 U.S. Code § 360l - Notification of Defect; Repair; Replacement; or Refund
perturbation.law_explanation: 21 U.S.C. § 360l requires manufacturers to notify the FDA upon discovery of a defect in a device (which could be considered analogous to the 'Product' in this agreement if it involves a medical device or drug product). This implies a duty of manufacturers to investigate and act responsibly upon discovering such defects. By allowing one party to unilaterally decide on a recall 'without informing the other,' the modified text may impede the full investigation and reporting required under the law, potentially hindering the FDA's oversight and ability to ensure public safety.

perturbation.justification: The modified text removes the requirement for CARDAX and CAPSUGEL to consult with each other in good faith and make joint decisions regarding product recalls. This contradicts the implied duty to cooperate and potentially undermines consumer protection laws, as the initiating party can now unilaterally decide on a recall. Although Scraped Law Snippet 1 is a dead link and Scraped Law Snippet 2 is empty, the Perturbation Explanation and Law Explanation describe a situation where a manufacturer has a duty to investigate and act responsibly and that not informing the other party hinders investigation and reporting. By allowing unilateral action, the modified text deviates from legally recognized expectations of cooperation, which suggests a contradiction under the 'Misaligned Terminology Legal Contradiction' definition.
